title: primula vulgaris subsp. heterochroma
intro: Primula vulgaris subsp. heterochroma, also known as the yellow primrose or primrose of the Alps, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It is a perennial herb used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, due to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Primula vulgaris subsp. heterochroma include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, or other topical applications for medicinal purposes.
